items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: emulsion breaking in sauces. (set 2)
items.explanation: Core explanation: fat and water phases separate.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: difference between stock and broth. (set 2)
items.a: stock is simmered from bones; broth is from meat and seasoning
items.explanation: Core explanation: stock is simmered from bones; broth is from meat and seasoning.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: gelatinization in starch cooking. (set 2)
items.a: starch granules absorb water and thicken when heated
items.explanation: Core explanation: starch granules absorb water and thicken when heated.

items.q: In food science, what best defines 'denaturation of proteins'?
items.a: protein structure unfolds due to heat, acid, or agitation
options: protein structure unfolds due to heat, acid, or agitation
butterfat with milk solids and water removed
roasting uses dry heat; braising combines sear and moist heat
items.explanation: This concept is defined as: protein structure unfolds due to heat, acid, or agitation.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: autolyse in bread making. (set 2)
items.a: resting flour and water before kneading to improve dough development
items.explanation: Core explanation: resting flour and water before kneading to improve dough development.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: tempering chocolate purpose. (set 2)
items.a: stabilize cocoa butter crystals for gloss and snap
items.explanation: Core explanation: stabilize cocoa butter crystals for gloss and snap.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: smoke point meaning. (set 2)
items.a: temperature at which fat begins to visibly smoke and degrade
items.explanation: Core explanation: temperature at which fat begins to visibly smoke and degrade.
